content.question: To what extent would eliminating formal age-10/12 assignment to 6/8-year gimnázium tracks reduce between-school segregation if school-choice policies, within-school class-selection mechanisms, and residential segregation remain structurally unchanged?
content.reduction_path: Conduct matched-pair or quasi-experimental comparison of Hungarian regions or cohorts with varying degree of choice-restriction (assigned neighborhood schools vs. competitive application) while holding other factors constant, or model counterfactual SES/Roma distribution across schools under hypothetical elimination of formal tracks with choice policies held constant.
